instruction: Extract the exoplanet discovery data into a JSON object with keys: "planet_name", "host_star", "discovery_year", "distance_light_years", "orbital_period_days", "mass_earth_units", "potentially_habitable".
input: Kepler-452c was discovered in 2024, orbiting the star Kepler-452 approximately 1,400 light-years from Earth. The planet has a mass of 3.8 Earth masses and completes one orbit every 385 days. Scientists have flagged it as potentially habitable.

instruction: Extract the power plant data into a JSON object with keys: "plant_name", "operator", "country", "energy_source", "capacity_mw", "commissioned_year", "operational".
input: The Solara Sunrise facility, operated by GreenGrid Energy in Morocco, is a utility-scale solar power plant with a generation capacity of 580 MW. It was commissioned in 2022 and remains fully operational.

instruction: Extract campaign details into a JSON object with keys "campaign" (sub-keys: "campaign_id", "disease", "target_population", "launch_date") and "agency" (sub-keys: "name", "country", "funding_source").
